tests.testId: Regression BUG-004: proxy HTTP failure falls back once to same-provider local key
tests.description: Parameterized non-2xx responses include 503 PROVIDER_KEY_MISSING and another non-2xx status; production providerFetch produces exact health, keyless proxy, then one same-provider direct order and returns the direct host's distinct structure.
tests.command: node --test --test-name-pattern="Regression BUG-004: proxy HTTP failure falls back once to same-provider local key" tests/provider-credentials.functional.mjs

tests.testId: Regression BUG-004: proxy transport rejection falls back once to same-provider local key
tests.description: A proxy provider-route fetch rejection reaches exactly one direct attempt for the requested provider; the raw transport error is absent from result and rejection surfaces.
tests.command: node --test --test-name-pattern="Regression BUG-004: proxy transport rejection falls back once to same-provider local key" tests/provider-credentials.functional.mjs

tests.testId: Regression BUG-004: proxy timeout rejection falls back once to same-provider local key
tests.description: An additive loadRldata timer/abort injection observes the real fetchT timeout contract deterministically; one same-provider direct attempt follows without a wall-clock third-party call.
tests.command: node --test --test-name-pattern="Regression BUG-004: proxy timeout rejection falls back once to same-provider local key" tests/provider-credentials.functional.mjs
tests.externalBoundary: deterministic injected fetch plus test-only timer/abort seam

tests.testId: Regression BUG-004: proxy JSON decode failure falls back once to same-provider local key
tests.description: A proxy HTTP success whose json method rejects reaches exactly one same-provider direct attempt and returns its distinct parsed structure.
tests.command: node --test --test-name-pattern="Regression BUG-004: proxy JSON decode failure falls back once to same-provider local key" tests/provider-credentials.functional.mjs

tests.testId: Regression BUG-004: fallback never crosses provider or retries
tests.description: With multiple provider keys present, only the requested provider's registered host and key parameter are used; one proxy route and at most one direct attempt occur.
tests.command: node --test --test-name-pattern="Regression BUG-004: fallback never crosses provider or retries" tests/provider-credentials.functional.mjs

tests.testId: Regression BUG-004: no same-provider key fails closed without disclosure
tests.description: Proxy 503 with no requested-provider key makes zero direct requests and rejects exactly PROVIDER_KEY_MISSING:finnhub; URLs, response body, other-provider keys, provider status/access, tool reads, errors, and captured diagnostics disclose no key.
tests.command: node --test --test-name-pattern="Regression BUG-004: no same-provider key fails closed without disclosure" tests/provider-credentials.functional.mjs

tests.testId: SCN-BUG004-003 force-local uses the shared direct provider path
tests.description: Force-local bypasses the proxy provider route, uses registered direct-host behavior, and performs exactly one direct attempt through the same production path.
tests.command: node --test --test-name-pattern="SCN-BUG004-003 force-local uses the shared direct provider path" tests/provider-credentials.functional.mjs

tests.testId: SCN-BUG002-004 fail-closed transport and prototype-safe unknown providers
tests.description: Unknown, empty, toString, constructor, __proto__, and prototype identifiers fail before config or transport access and leave built-in prototypes and transport history unchanged.
tests.command: node --test --test-name-pattern="SCN-BUG002-004 fail-closed transport and prototype-safe unknown providers" tests/provider-credentials.unit.mjs

tests.testId: SCN-BUG004-003 force-local status stays masked with a reachable local proxy
tests.description: A real browser and loopback HTTP health server exercise the data-settings integration without page/context interception; force-local renders masked local-tier status and no credential reaches DOM, status, tool reads, diagnostics, URL, cookie, or history.
tests.command: npx --no-install playwright test tests/provider-fallback-status.spec.mjs --config=playwright.config.mjs --project=system-chrome --grep "SCN-BUG004-003 force-local status stays masked with a reachable local proxy" --reporter=list
tests.liveBoundary: local browser/application plus loopback HTTP proxy-health server

tests.description: Protected option snapshots, snapshot producer, both snapshot-first consumers, and completed BUG-002 artifacts contain no implementation byte change.
tests.command: git diff --exit-code -- data/options scripts/fetch-options.mjs options-structure-lab.html options-flow-feed-lab.html specs/_bugs/BUG-002-two-tier-provider-access

tests.description: Existing loader defaults, isolated storage, injected-fetch ordering, provider configuration, lifecycle persistence, and force-local setup remain compatible after additive support seams.

The existing editor, proxy status, force-local, clear-all, disclosure, and prototype-defense browser checks remain green; context.route interception makes this functional browser coverage, not live transport or e2e-ui evidence.
The complete Research Lab selftest remains green without option snapshot, provider registry, or completed BUG-002 behavior drift.

tests.testId: Broad Regression E2E UI: complete BUG-004 no-interception browser file
tests.description: Runs the complete no-interception BUG-004 browser file without a title filter, protecting the local browser/application and loopback-health surface without claiming external-provider transport.
tests.liveBoundary: local browser/application plus loopback HTTP proxy-health server

applicability.unit: required for provider identity and prototype defenses
applicability.functional: required and authoritative for deterministic third-party transport-boundary behavior
applicability.integration: not-applicable: Research Lab has no owned provider service tier
applicability.ui-unit: not-applicable: no component framework exists; the intercepted legacy browser file is classified functional
applicability.e2e-api: not-applicable: Research Lab has no service API
applicability.e2e-ui: required for the scenario-specific TP-09 and broad file-level TP-14 local data-settings and masked-status integrations
applicability.stress: not-applicable: no latency or repetition contract changes
applicability.load: not-applicable: no concurrency or capacity contract changes

classification.reason: The production rldata.js path is real, but true external HTTP responses are deterministically injected or intercepted; these rows do not prove live provider transport.
classification.browserE2eBoundary: Real local browser/application and loopback HTTP health server only; no external provider E2E claim.

excluded: all tool HTML except read-only execution of index.html#data-settings
provider registry additions, removals, host changes, or key-parameter changes

injected fetch call order remains observable
local and session storage remain isolated per test
startStaticServer signature and static-file behavior remain unchanged
provider config and force-local setup remain compatible
